network_sdks.name: mesh-network-sdk
network_sdks.description: Self-healing mesh topology with OLSR-inspired routing for IoT and edge clusters.

network_sdks.name: p2p-content-sdk
network_sdks.description: Content-addressed peer-to-peer distribution with Merkle verification.

network_sdks.name: blockchain-consensus-sdk
network_sdks.description: Pluggable BFT consensus engine with hot-swap validator sets.

network_sdks.name: dag-ledger-sdk
network_sdks.description: Directed acyclic graph ledger for high-throughput asynchronous consensus.

network_sdks.name: federated-learning-sdk
network_sdks.description: Decentralized ML training with differential privacy and secure aggregation.

network_sdks.name: iot-sensor-mesh-sdk
network_sdks.description: Low-power sensor mesh with time-synchronized data fusion.

network_sdks.name: cdn-edge-sdk
network_sdks.description: Edge-compute CDN with cache invalidation and push-based prefetch.

network_sdks.name: neural-inference-network-sdk
network_sdks.description: Distributed neural inference with pipeline parallelism and KV-cache sharding.

network_sdks.name: zero-trust-overlay-sdk
network_sdks.description: Identity-aware overlay with mTLS, attestation, and micro-segmentation.

network_sdks.name: pub-sub-event-sdk
network_sdks.description: Exactly-once event bus with ordered streams and consumer groups.

network_sdks.name: gossip-protocol-sdk
network_sdks.description: Epidemic gossip with anti-entropy and vector-clock reconciliation.

network_sdks.name: byzantine-fault-tolerant-sdk
network_sdks.description: BFT state machine replication with threshold signatures.

network_sdks.name: sharded-state-sdk
network_sdks.description: Consistent-hashing state shards with cross-shard transaction atomicity.

network_sdks.name: cross-chain-bridge-sdk
network_sdks.description: Trust-minimized bridge with light-client verification and relayer auctions.

network_sdks.name: time-synchronisation-sdk
network_sdks.description: Sub-microsecond clock sync with Byzantine fault-tolerant time master election.

network_sdks.name: anonymous-mixnet-sdk
network_sdks.description: Layered encryption mixnet with Sphinx packet format and cover traffic.

network_sdks.name: storage-dht-sdk
network_sdks.description: Mutable DHT with erasure-coded storage and proof-of-retrievability.

network_sdks.name: compute-grid-sdk
network_sdks.description: Volunteer compute grid with task verification and reputation-weighted scheduling.

network_sdks.name: identity-did-sdk
network_sdks.description: Self-sovereign identity with verifiable credentials and selective disclosure.

network_sdks.name: streaming-media-sdk
network_sdks.description: Adaptive bitrate streaming with WebRTC mesh and SRT relay fallback.

llm_oses.name: microkernel-llm-os
llm_oses.description: Microkernel where LLM inference runs as a privileged server with capability-based IPC.

llm_oses.name: exokernel-llm-os
llm_oses.description: Exokernel exposing raw GPU/TPU hardware to user-space LLM runtimes with secure multiplexing.

llm_oses.name: unikernel-llm-os
llm_oses.description: Single-address-space unikernel optimized for single-tenant LLM inference on cloud hypervisors.

llm_oses.name: distributed-llm-os
llm_oses.description: OS spanning multiple nodes where processes migrate toward GPU-equipped hosts automatically.

llm_oses.name: real-time-llm-os
llm_oses.description: Hard real-time scheduler guaranteeing inference latency bounds for safety-critical LLM outputs.

llm_oses.name: capability-based-llm-os
llm_oses.description: Capability system where file handles, GPU contexts, and model weights are all capabilities.

llm_oses.name: event-driven-llm-os
llm_oses.description: Kernel dispatch is entirely event-driven; LLM prompts are high-priority kernel events.

llm_oses.name: actor-model-llm-os
llm_oses.description: Every process is an actor; LLM tokens are messages with backpressure and supervision trees.

llm_oses.name: dataflow-llm-os
llm_oses.description: OS structured as a dataflow graph; LLM layers are nodes, tensors flow on edges.

llm_oses.name: reactive-llm-os
llm_oses.description: Reactive streams at kernel level; LLM outputs propagate as observable hot streams.

llm_oses.name: container-native-llm-os
llm_oses.description: Kernel is a container orchestrator; every LLM inference is a sandboxed container with cgroup v3.

llm_oses.name: serverless-llm-os
llm_oses.description: Cold-start optimized kernel for ephemeral LLM functions; keeps hot model weights in kernel-resident cache.

llm_oses.name: peer-to-peer-llm-os
llm_oses.description: OS without central servers; LLM weights are erasure-coded across peer devices.

llm_oses.name: mesh-llm-os
llm_oses.description: Mesh-topology OS for edge clusters; inference workloads route through nearest GPU hop.

llm_oses.name: hierarchical-llm-os
llm_oses.description: Ring-0 hosts small model, delegates to ring-1 medium, ring-2 large model via hierarchical calls.

llm_oses.name: flat-address-llm-os
llm_oses.description: Single global 64-bit address space; LLM KV-cache is memory-mapped across the entire cluster.

llm_oses.name: object-capability-llm-os
llm_oses.description: Object-capability security; each LLM token generation requires a capability delegation chain.

llm_oses.name: language-based-llm-os
llm_oses.description: OS implemented in a linearly-typed language where memory safety proves tensor shape correctness.

llm_oses.name: virtualized-llm-os
llm_oses.description: Type-1 hypervisor hosting para-virtualized LLM guests with direct GPU passthrough.

llm_oses.name: baremetal-llm-os
llm_oses.description: No abstractions; user code directly programs GPU DMA rings and token decode loops.

llm_oses.name: mobile-llm-os
llm_oses.description: Battery-aware scheduler quantizing models to NPU and falling back to CPU on thermal throttling.

llm_oses.name: embedded-llm-os
llm_oses.description: Sub-MB kernel for MCUs; runs TinyML models with on-device fine-tuning via federated delta.

llm_oses.name: edge-llm-os
llm_oses.description: Gateway OS caching model shards and serving them to low-power clients over LoRa/WiFi.

llm_oses.name: cloud-llm-os
llm_oses.description: Multi-tenant kernel with GPU time-slicing and spot-preemptible LLM inference jobs.

llm_oses.name: hybrid-llm-os
llm_oses.description: Seamlessly offloads between on-device NPU, edge GPU, and cloud TPU based on latency cost.

llm_oses.name: deterministic-llm-os
llm_oses.description: Fully reproducible kernel state; same prompt always produces identical token sequence and side effects.

llm_oses.name: probabilistic-llm-os
llm_oses.description: Kernel schedules based on expected information gain; high-entropy prompts get priority.

llm_oses.name: self-hosting-llm-os
llm_oses.description: OS can rewrite its own kernel source via LLM and hot-reload without reboot.

llm_oses.name: recursive-llm-os
llm_oses.description: OS runs a smaller OS inside itself to validate speculative LLM outputs before committing to host state.

llm_oses.name: symbiotic-llm-os
llm_oses.description: Human and LLM share equal scheduling rights; the kernel arbitrates CPU time between human tasks and model thoughts.
